title: turnera ulmifolia var. elegans
intro: Turnera ulmifolia var. elegans, also known as Mexican chamomile, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its yellow flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Turnera ulmifolia var. elegans include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to soothe digestive issues and reduce stress.
